title: pact.decisions
description: Canonical wire schema for the pact.decisions Kafka topic, mirroring internal/kafka/producer.go DecisionEvent. Field names and required/optional status are frozen per ENGINEERING.md - pact-audit pins this shape. additionalProperties is false at every level so an accidental field rename or addition fails validation instead of silently passing through as unrecognised JSONB.

event_uuid.description: Autopopulated by PublishDecision when the caller leaves it empty; always present on the wire.

session_id.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the bearer token - never the token itself.

decision.description: Closed set - pipeline.DecisionKind (internal/pipeline/types.go) only ever produces these two values.

reason.description: Free-form diagnostic reason string (e.g. filter_hostile, classifier_tagged, external_ref_blocked). Open set by design - new reason codes are added without a schema change; engine attribution is derived from the reason prefix, not enumerated here.

engine.description: Closed set derived from the reason-prefix switches in check.emitDecisionEvent, proxy.emitProxyDecisionEvent, and middleware.auditCheckRejections: filter, classifier, consensus, redactor, policy, sandbox (check-path indirect-injection block), gateway (pre-pipeline rejection, e.g. body-too-large, decode failure) and cel (PACT-335 tier-3 CEL rule escalated allow to block).

traffic_source.description: Caller-declared attribution of synthetic vs real traffic (e.g. "benchmark"). This is attribution, not authorization - it carries no access-control weight and must never be used to grant or deny a request. Omitted entirely when the caller did not declare a traffic source.

contentRef.description: Hash reference to the request content. Absent on requests whose body was never decoded (e.g. pre-pipeline rejections).

sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the request content.

verdict.description: Closed set per pipeline.Decision.FilterVerdict doc comment (internal/pipeline/types.go).

shadow.description: True when the match was a dry-run shadow rule.

enforce_mode.description: Staged-enforcement mode (PACT-257 vector enforcement); pipeline.EnforceModeShadow / EnforceModeEnforce.

label.description: Model-reported label. Open set by design - pact-classifier owns the label vocabulary and can add heads without a gateway schema change.

engine.description: Free-form model/checkpoint tag (e.g. stub-v1, deberta-prompt-injection-v2@abcd1234). Open set - mirrors classifierpb.ClassifyResponse.model_version.

label.description: Open set - same per-backend label vocabulary as classifier.label.

consensusDecision.description: Present only when classifier_score < PACT_CONSENSUS_THRESHOLD (stage 2.5 ran).

label.description: Open set - same label vocabulary as classifier.label.

quorum_reached.description: No omitempty on the Go field - always present within an emitted consensus object, even when false.

mode.description: PACT-432. Present only when the vote ran as a background completion off the /v1/check hot path; absent (not "inline") when the vote ran inline - kafka.ConsensusDecision.Mode is only ever set to "shadow".

skipped_reason.description: PACT-432. Machine-readable cause of skipped=true: consensus_timeout, consensus_error (shadow-mode vote failures), or shadow_saturated (completion pool full). Open set by design, and not populated on the pre-existing inline-mode transport-error fail-open (skipped=true with no reason) to keep that path's wire output unchanged.

label.description: Detector name (e.g. EMAIL, CREDIT_CARD). Open set - the redactor owns the detector vocabulary.

verdict.description: Closed set per pipeline.Decision.RedactorVerdict doc comment (internal/pipeline/types.go).

Closed set per pipeline.Decision.PolicyVerdict doc comment. The empty string is also a valid pipeline value (skipped - no token presented) but that case never constructs a policy sub-object at all, so it never appears on the wire.

tool_mitigation_mode.description: Closed set per pipeline.Decision.ToolMitigationMode doc comment (PACT-304/PACT-247). The empty string is also valid but is dropped by omitempty, so it never appears on the wire.

spotlightDecision.description: Present when spotlight_chunks were wrapped, or when stripping was detected in an output check.

source_count.description: No omitempty on the Go field - always present within an emitted spotlight object; 0 is distinct from absent.

marker_in_output.description: True when a kind=output content check contained the spotlight closing-marker prefix.

source.description: Caller-supplied provenance tag for the reference (e.g. rag:1, tool:web). Open set - defined by the /v1/check caller, not the gateway.

externalRefDecision.description: Indirect-injection sandbox result (PACT-236). Present only on the check path when the request declared external references and the sandbox ran; the proxy path never sets this.

source.description: Constant provenance tag; no omitempty on the Go field.

routingDecision.description: Which upstream served a proxied request (PACT-336). Set only on the proxy output-scan event, after a successful forward; never carries base_url or an API key.

upstream_id.description: Stable per-account upstream id; empty means the global fallback upstream.

provider.description: Admin-configured upstream provider tag (e.g. openai, openai-compatible). Open set - operators can register arbitrary provider names.

celDecision.description: Decisive tier-3 CEL rule outcome (PACT-335). Present only when the CEL stage ran and either at least one active rule fired or the stage recorded a skip reason - a stage that ran and evaluated zero rules to true, with no budget exhaustion, is omitted entirely.

outcome.description: Closed set - celeval.Outcome* constants. Only "block" ever escalates decision/reason; "redact" and "log" are audit-only (CEL runs after the redactor stage, so there is nothing left to redact).

fired_count.description: Total number of active rules whose expression evaluated to true, which may exceed 1 even though only the decisive (highest-severity) rule is named above.

skipped_reason.description: Closed set - celeval.SkipReason* constants. Present only when the stage could not fully evaluate every active rule (fail-open): cel_stage_timeout/cel_rule_timeout are budget exhaustion, cel_rule_error covers both a CEL runtime error and an expression that evaluated cleanly to a non-boolean value. Absent when every active rule was attempted regardless of outcome.
